samples.question: If I want to withdraw from a course after seeing my workload, what rules or deadlines should I check before I make the decision?
samples.answer_text: Before withdrawing from a course in an ICU setting, you must verify the following: 1. **Registration Change Period**: You cannot withdraw after the Registration Change period has ended. The Registration Change period has already over, so no registration change is approved. [1] 2. **Late Registration Procedure**: If you missed the Registration Period, you must complete registration through the Late Registration procedure. You must pay the Late Registration Fee (3,000 yen) before proceeding to the registration interview with your advisor. [3] 3. **Course Eligibility and Requirements**: You must have Pre-Registered for the course and made a decision to take it before the term starts. You may be required to take the introductory course before registering for the intermediate course. [4] 5. **Health and Physical Education Courses**: Health and Physical Education Courses are recommended, but you must complete the regular registration procedures on Registration day. [2] 6. **Final Confirmation**: After registration and registration change, please confirm that the courses you wish to add and/or cancel is reflected correctly via the Registration Website "Registration Results". samples.gold_answer: Check the Registration Change deadline — no registration changes are approved after the Registration Change period ends and no retroactive credit will be given; failing to drop a course (i.e., remaining absent without completing the drop) can result in an F [1]. Be sure to complete the Registration Change properly by clicking the “SAVE” button and confirming your Registration Results [2]. If you miss the regular period, verify Late Registration procedures, deadlines, advisor approval, and the late‑registration fee (e.g., ¥3,000) [3]. Also note that a Registration Withdrawal (which withdraws ALL courses) requires dean permission for illness/unavoidable reasons [1].

samples.question: I am trying to plan my major requirements and study abroad at the same time. Where should I look to confirm whether courses or credits will count toward my ICU requirements?

To find the correct official support page, use the following steps: 1. **Identify the Office:** Locate the **Learning Accessibility Services (LAS)** office on your campus website. 2. **Navigate to the Support Page:** Go to the LAS office page (e.g., `https://office.icu.ac.jp/ctl/snss/for_students.html`) and look for the section titled **"Reasonable Accommodations"** or **"合理的配慮について"**. 3. **Review Key Information:** On this page, you will find: * **Request Process:** Steps 1–4 for applying (e.g., submit medical certificate, write a letter, request accommodation plan, notify professors). * **Support Contents:** Examples of accommodations provided (e.g., extended exam time, note-taking, text-to-speech, modified textbooks, sign-language interpreters, transportation). * **Deadline:** For new requests, the deadline is the **Week 4 of the current semester** (e.g., Week 4 of Term 2). * **Contact:** Phone, email, and/or office hours for LAS staff. **Summary:** * **Where to start:** Your course instructor and the Learning Accessibility Services (LAS) office. * **What page to look for:** The LAS office page containing the "Reasonable Accommodations" section.
samples.gold_answer: Start by consulting your course instructor and contacting Learning Accessibility Services (学修アクセシビリティ支援室 / LAS).
